Center: ATOMKI Nuclear Reaction Data Group, Debrecen, Hungary
ref: Jour: Nucl. Instrum. Methods in Physics Res., Sect.B, Vol.563, p.165670 (2025)
title: Measurement of activation cross section of alpha-particle induced nuclear reactions on dysprosium

x4freetext: Measurement of activation cross section of
alpha-particle induced nuclear reactions on dysprosium

x4codes.hlp: Institute for Nuclear Research (ATOMKI), Debrecen, Hungary
RIKEN (Inst. of Physical and Chemical Research), Wako, Japan

x4codes.shortRef: Jour: Nucl. Instrum. Methods in Physics Res., Sect.B, Vol.563, p.165670 (2025)

x4codes.hlp: RIKEN (Inst. of Physical and Chemical Research), Wako, Japan

x4freetext: Natural metalic Dy foil, thickness of 21 um.
Cross sections were measured by using charged
particle activation method.
Activity measurement of the reaction products
was done by Gamma-spectrometry based on HPGe detectors.
Stacked foils targets was irradiated with
alpha-particle beam of 50.5 MeV, and 200 nA for 1 h.
Energy degradation was calculated by using the
polynomial approximation of Andersen ad Ziegler.
The irradiation was done on an extracted beam of
the AVF cyclotron of RIKEN.
The activities of the produced radionuclides were
measured nondestructively without chemical separation.

x4codes.shortRef: Jour: Nuclear Data Sheets, Vol.148, p.338 (2018)

x4freetext: The uncertainty on the median energy in the
foils includes the cumulative effects of uncertainty of
incident beam energy, uncertainty of energy loss of
each foil due to thickness variation and straggling
effects. Uncertainty of the primary incident beam
energy was +- 0.2MeV which increased up to +- 0.8 MeV
in the last Dy foil of the stack.
The uncertainty on the cross section values
are estimated in the  standard way as the square root
of the quadratic summation of the independently
contributing parameters. Uncertainty of time parameters
is not included.
number of target atoms/cm2 (3.0%)
beam intensity including correction(5.0%)
gamma intensities (0.5-32.5%)
detector efficiency (3.0-6.0%)
statistical and peak fitting (1.-7.%)
The experimental results were compared to the
results of theoretical model code, taken from TENDL
data library.
TS.
